testCases.title: The user should be able to register successfully when valid unique email and compliant password are provided
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration screen
The email address used is not already registered in the system
Password complexity rules have been confirmed by PM/Dev

steps.content: Navigate to the Registration screen
steps.expected: Registration screen is displayed with email and password input fields

steps.content: Enter a valid, unique email address in the email field (e.g., newuser@example.com)
steps.expected: Email is accepted and displayed in the field without inline errors

steps.content: Enter a compliant password in the password field (e.g., meets all complexity rules)
steps.expected: Password is masked by default and accepted without inline errors

steps.content: Tap the Register / Submit button
steps.expected: Form is submitted; a loading or processing indicator is shown

steps.content: Observe the post-registration state
steps.expected: A success state is shown (e.g., confirmation message or onboarding screen); a confirmation email is sent to the registered address

testCases.expectedResult: A new user account is created successfully. The user sees a post-registration success state and receives a confirmation email.

testCases.title: The system should prevent registration when an already-registered email address is submitted
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration screen
The email address used is already registered in the system

steps.content: Navigate to the Registration screen
steps.expected: Registration screen is displayed

steps.content: Enter an email address that already exists in the system
steps.expected: Email is entered in the field

steps.content: Enter a compliant password in the password field
steps.expected: Password is entered and masked

steps.content: Tap the Register / Submit button
steps.expected: Form submission is attempted

steps.content: Observe the system response
steps.expected: An error message is displayed indicating the email is already in use; no duplicate account is created

testCases.expectedResult: The system rejects the registration attempt and displays an appropriate error. No duplicate account is created in the system.

testCases.title: The registration form should block submission when required fields are left empty
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration screen

steps.content: Navigate to the Registration screen
steps.expected: Registration screen is displayed with all required fields empty

steps.content: Leave both the email and password fields empty
steps.expected: Fields remain empty; no input is entered

steps.content: Tap the Register / Submit button
steps.expected: Submission is blocked; inline validation errors are displayed for all empty required fields, or the submit button remains disabled

testCases.expectedResult: The form does not submit. Inline error messages or a disabled submit button indicate that required fields must be filled.

testCases.title: The system should handle registration gracefully when the network drops mid-submission
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration screen with valid data entered
Ability to simulate network interruption during the test

steps.content: Navigate to the Registration screen
steps.expected: Registration screen is displayed

steps.content: Enter a valid unique email and compliant password
steps.expected: Fields are populated without inline errors

steps.content: Tap the Register / Submit button and immediately disable network connectivity on the device
steps.expected: Form submission is initiated; network is interrupted

steps.content: Observe the system response after the network drops
steps.expected: A clear error message is displayed (e.g., 'No internet connection. Please try again.'); the app does not crash or hang indefinitely

steps.content: Restore network connectivity and attempt to submit the form again
steps.expected: The form can be resubmitted; registration completes successfully

testCases.expectedResult: The app handles the network interruption gracefully with a user-friendly error message. No partial or corrupt account is created. The user can retry successfully once connectivity is restored.

testCases.title: The system should prevent rapid repeated registration attempts when the same email is submitted multiple times in quick succession
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration screen
Rate limiting or debounce is implemented on the backend

steps.content: Navigate to the Registration screen
steps.expected: Registration screen is displayed

steps.content: Enter a valid unique email and compliant password
steps.expected: Fields are populated without inline errors

steps.content: Tap the Register / Submit button multiple times in rapid succession
steps.expected: Only one submission is processed; subsequent taps are debounced or the button is disabled after the first tap

steps.content: Observe the system response
steps.expected: Only one account creation request is sent to the server; no duplicate accounts are created; a rate-limit or throttle error is shown if the threshold is exceeded

testCases.expectedResult: The system prevents duplicate submissions from rapid tapping. Only one registration request is processed per attempt.

testCases.title: The registration form should sanitize input correctly when special characters or SQL-like strings are entered in the email or password fields
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration screen

steps.content: Navigate to the Registration screen
steps.expected: Registration screen is displayed

steps.content: Enter a SQL injection string in the email field (e.g., `' OR '1'='1`)
steps.expected: Input is accepted in the field as plain text

steps.content: Enter a script injection string in the password field (e.g., `<script>alert('xss')</script>`)
steps.expected: Input is accepted in the field as plain text and masked

steps.content: Tap the Register / Submit button
steps.expected: The form either rejects the invalid email format with an inline error or sanitizes the input server-side; no script execution occurs; no database error is exposed to the user

testCases.expectedResult: The system safely handles malicious input. No SQL injection or XSS vulnerability is triggered. The user sees a validation error for invalid email format, not a system error.

testCases.title: The user should be redirected to Google OAuth when the 'Continue with Google' option is selected
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Login screen
Device has an active internet connection
Google OAuth test credentials are available

steps.content: Navigate to the Login screen
steps.expected: Login screen is displayed with a 'Continue with Google' option

steps.content: Tap the 'Continue with Google' button
steps.expected: The app initiates the Google OAuth flow; the user is redirected to the Google sign-in screen or a Google account chooser

testCases.expectedResult: The Google OAuth screen or account chooser is displayed, allowing the user to select or enter their Google credentials.

testCases.title: The user should be successfully authenticated and logged in when valid Google credentials are provided via SSO
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Login screen
Device has an active internet connection
Valid Google test account credentials are available

steps.content: Navigate to the Login screen
steps.expected: Login screen is displayed

steps.content: Tap the 'Continue with Google' button
steps.expected: Google OAuth flow is initiated

steps.content: Select or enter valid Google account credentials and approve the OAuth consent
steps.expected: Google authenticates the user and returns an authorization token to the app

steps.content: Observe the app state after OAuth completes
steps.expected: The user is logged in and navigated to the main dashboard or home screen within 3 seconds

testCases.expectedResult: The user is successfully authenticated via Google SSO and lands on the post-login screen. Login completes within the 3-second SLA.

testCases.title: The system should display an appropriate error when the Google OAuth flow is cancelled by the user
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Login screen
Device has an active internet connection

steps.content: Navigate to the Login screen
steps.expected: Login screen is displayed

steps.content: Tap the 'Continue with Google' button
steps.expected: Google OAuth flow is initiated

steps.content: Cancel or dismiss the Google sign-in screen (e.g., tap Back or Cancel)
steps.expected: The OAuth flow is cancelled

steps.content: Observe the app state
steps.expected: The user is returned to the Login screen; an appropriate message is displayed (e.g., 'Sign-in was cancelled'); the app does not crash

testCases.expectedResult: The app handles the OAuth cancellation gracefully. The user is returned to the Login screen with a non-blocking informational message.

testCases.title: The system should fail gracefully when Google services are unavailable during SSO login
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Login screen
Google services are simulated as unavailable (e.g., via network block or mock)

steps.content: Navigate to the Login screen
steps.expected: Login screen is displayed

steps.content: Tap the 'Continue with Google' button while Google services are unavailable
steps.expected: The OAuth flow is attempted

steps.content: Observe the system response
steps.expected: A user-friendly error message is displayed (e.g., 'Unable to connect to Google. Please try again later.'); the app does not crash or hang

testCases.expectedResult: The app handles Google service unavailability gracefully with a clear error message. The user can return to the Login screen and try again.

testCases.title: The system should handle token refresh correctly when the Google session expires mid-flow
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User has initiated the Google SSO flow
Ability to simulate Google session token expiry (e.g., via mock or expired test token)

steps.content: Initiate the Google SSO login flow
steps.expected: Google OAuth screen is displayed

steps.content: Simulate the Google session token expiring before the flow completes
steps.expected: Token expiry is triggered mid-flow

steps.content: Observe the system response
steps.expected: The system either automatically attempts a token refresh or displays an appropriate error prompting the user to sign in again; the app does not crash

testCases.expectedResult: The app handles the expired Google token gracefully. Either the token is refreshed transparently or the user is prompted to re-authenticate without data loss or a crash.

testCases.title: The system should send a password reset email when a registered email address is submitted via the Forgot Password flow
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Login screen
The email address used is registered in the system
Test email inbox is configured and accessible (e.g., Mailosaur)

steps.content: Navigate to the Login screen
steps.expected: Login screen is displayed

steps.content: Tap the 'Forgot Password' link or button
steps.expected: Forgot Password screen is displayed with an email input field

steps.content: Enter a valid registered email address in the email field
steps.expected: Email is entered without inline errors

steps.content: Tap the Submit / Send Reset Email button
steps.expected: A confirmation message is displayed (e.g., 'If this email is registered, a reset link has been sent')

steps.content: Check the test email inbox for the password reset email
steps.expected: A password reset email is received within an acceptable delivery time; the email contains a valid reset link

testCases.expectedResult: A password reset email is delivered to the registered email address. The app displays a confirmation message without revealing whether the email is registered.

testCases.title: The user should be able to set a new password when a valid and unexpired reset link is used
preconditions: A valid, unexpired password reset email has been received
The reset link has not been previously used
Password complexity rules have been confirmed by PM/Dev

steps.content: Open the password reset email and tap the reset link
steps.expected: The app or browser opens the password reset screen with a new password input field

steps.content: Enter a new compliant password in the new password field
steps.expected: Password is entered and masked; no inline errors are shown

steps.content: Confirm the new password in the confirm password field (if applicable)
steps.expected: Confirm password field matches the new password

steps.content: Tap the Submit / Reset Password button
steps.expected: The new password is submitted

steps.content: Observe the system response
steps.expected: A success message is displayed; the user is redirected to the Login screen or automatically logged in

steps.content: Attempt to log in using the new password
steps.expected: Login is successful with the new password

testCases.expectedResult: The user successfully resets their password using a valid reset link and can log in with the new credentials.

testCases.title: The reset link should be invalid and rejected when accessed after the token expiry window
preconditions: A password reset email has been received
The reset token expiry window has elapsed (exact duration to be confirmed with Dev)
Test environment supports token expiry simulation

steps.content: Wait for the reset token to expire (or simulate expiry in the test environment)
steps.expected: Token expiry window has passed

steps.content: Open the password reset email and tap the expired reset link
steps.expected: The app or browser attempts to open the reset screen

steps.content: Observe the system response
steps.expected: An error message is displayed indicating the link has expired (e.g., 'This reset link has expired. Please request a new one.'); the user is not able to set a new password

testCases.expectedResult: The expired reset link is rejected. The user sees a clear error message and is prompted to request a new reset link.

testCases.title: The reset link should be invalidated after a single successful use
preconditions: A valid password reset email has been received
The user has already successfully used the reset link to set a new password

steps.content: Use the reset link to successfully set a new password
steps.expected: Password is reset successfully

steps.content: Attempt to open the same reset link again
steps.expected: The app or browser attempts to open the reset screen

steps.content: Observe the system response
steps.expected: An error message is displayed indicating the link has already been used (e.g., 'This reset link is no longer valid.'); the user cannot reset the password again using the same link

testCases.expectedResult: The reset link is invalidated after one successful use. Reusing the link results in an appropriate error message.

testCases.title: The system should reject the password reset when the new password does not meet complexity requirements
preconditions: A valid, unexpired password reset link has been opened
Password complexity rules have been confirmed by PM/Dev

steps.content: Open a valid, unexpired password reset link
steps.expected: Password reset screen is displayed

steps.content: Enter a non-compliant password (e.g., too short, missing required character types)
steps.expected: Password is entered in the field

steps.content: Tap the Submit / Reset Password button
steps.expected: Submission is attempted

steps.content: Observe the system response
steps.expected: An inline error message is displayed describing the password complexity requirements; the password is not reset

testCases.expectedResult: The system rejects the non-compliant password and displays a clear inline error message. The password is not changed.

testCases.title: The system should not reveal whether an email is registered when a non-existent email is submitted via Forgot Password
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Forgot Password screen
The email address used is NOT registered in the system

steps.content: Navigate to the Forgot Password screen
steps.expected: Forgot Password screen is displayed

steps.content: Enter an email address that is not registered in the system
steps.expected: Email is entered in the field

steps.content: Tap the Submit / Send Reset Email button
steps.expected: Submission is processed

steps.content: Observe the system response
steps.expected: The same generic confirmation message is displayed as for a registered email (e.g., 'If this email is registered, a reset link has been sent'); no error indicating the email does not exist is shown

testCases.expectedResult: The system displays a generic confirmation message regardless of whether the email is registered, preventing user enumeration.

testCases.title: The system should handle multiple password reset requests gracefully when the same email is submitted in quick succession
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Forgot Password screen
The email address used is registered in the system

steps.content: Navigate to the Forgot Password screen
steps.expected: Forgot Password screen is displayed

steps.content: Enter a registered email address and tap Submit multiple times in quick succession
steps.expected: Multiple submission attempts are made

steps.content: Observe the system response
steps.expected: The system either throttles the requests and shows a rate-limit message, or only sends one reset email; the app does not crash

steps.content: Check the test email inbox
steps.expected: Only one or a limited number of reset emails are received; the inbox is not flooded

testCases.expectedResult: The system handles rapid repeated reset requests gracefully. Rate limiting prevents email flooding, and the user sees appropriate feedback.

testCases.title: The error message should display an invalid email format error when the user enters an email without the '@' symbol
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration or Login screen

steps.content: Navigate to the Registration or Login screen
steps.expected: Screen is displayed with an email input field

steps.content: Tap the email input field
steps.expected: Field is focused and keyboard is displayed

steps.content: Enter an email address without the '@' symbol (e.g., 'invalidemail.com')
steps.expected: Text is entered in the field

steps.content: Tap outside the field or attempt to submit the form
steps.expected: An inline error message is displayed indicating the email format is invalid (e.g., 'Please enter a valid email address')

testCases.expectedResult: An inline validation error is displayed for the email field. The form does not submit with an invalid email format.

testCases.title: The error message should display an invalid email format error when the user enters an email without a domain extension
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration or Login screen

steps.content: Navigate to the Registration or Login screen
steps.expected: Screen is displayed with an email input field

steps.content: Enter an email address missing a domain extension (e.g., 'user@domain')
steps.expected: Text is entered in the field

steps.content: Tap outside the field or attempt to submit the form
steps.expected: An inline error message is displayed indicating the email format is invalid

testCases.expectedResult: An inline validation error is displayed for the email field. The form does not submit.

testCases.title: The form should display a password complexity error when the password does not meet the defined complexity rules
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration screen
Password complexity rules have been confirmed by PM/Dev

steps.content: Navigate to the Registration screen
steps.expected: Registration screen is displayed

steps.content: Enter a valid email address in the email field
steps.expected: Email is accepted without errors

steps.content: Enter a non-compliant password in the password field (e.g., too short or missing required character types)
steps.expected: Password is entered and masked

steps.content: Tap outside the password field or attempt to submit the form
steps.expected: An inline error message is displayed describing the unmet password complexity requirements

testCases.expectedResult: An inline password complexity error is displayed. The form does not submit until the password meets all complexity requirements.

testCases.title: The submit button should remain disabled or block submission when required fields are empty on the Login screen
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Login screen

steps.content: Navigate to the Login screen
steps.expected: Login screen is displayed with email and password fields empty

steps.content: Leave both the email and password fields empty
steps.expected: No input is entered in either field

steps.content: Observe the state of the Login / Submit button
steps.expected: The button is visually disabled, or tapping it triggers inline validation errors for empty required fields

steps.content: Tap the Login / Submit button
steps.expected: Form submission is blocked; no API call is made; inline errors are shown for empty fields

testCases.expectedResult: The form does not submit when required fields are empty. The submit button is disabled or inline errors are shown.

testCases.title: The password field should mask input by default and toggle visibility correctly when the show/hide icon is tapped
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration or Login screen
A show/hide password toggle icon is present on the password field

steps.content: Navigate to the Registration or Login screen
steps.expected: Screen is displayed with a password input field

steps.content: Tap the password field and enter a password
steps.expected: Password characters are masked (displayed as dots or asterisks) by default

steps.content: Tap the show/hide password toggle icon
steps.expected: Password characters are revealed in plain text

steps.content: Tap the show/hide password toggle icon again
steps.expected: Password characters are masked again

testCases.expectedResult: The password field masks input by default. The show/hide toggle correctly reveals and re-masks the password on each tap.

testCases.title: The email field should display a validation error when input exceeds the maximum character length
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration or Login screen
Maximum email character length is confirmed with Dev

steps.content: Navigate to the Registration or Login screen
steps.expected: Screen is displayed with an email input field

steps.content: Enter an email address that exceeds the maximum allowed character length
steps.expected: Input is either truncated at the max length or the full string is entered

steps.content: Attempt to submit the form or tap outside the field
steps.expected: An inline error message is displayed indicating the email is too long, or the field prevents input beyond the maximum length

testCases.expectedResult: The email field enforces a maximum character length. An appropriate error or input restriction is applied.

testCases.title: The form should behave correctly when autofill populates fields with mismatched data types
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
Device autofill is enabled and has saved credentials
User is on the Login or Registration screen

steps.content: Navigate to the Login or Registration screen
steps.expected: Screen is displayed with email and password fields

steps.content: Allow device autofill to populate the email field with a non-email value (e.g., a phone number or name)
steps.expected: Autofill populates the field with mismatched data

steps.content: Attempt to submit the form
steps.expected: Inline validation detects the invalid email format and displays an error; the form does not submit

testCases.expectedResult: The form correctly validates autofill-populated data. Mismatched data types trigger appropriate inline validation errors.

testCases.title: The user should be successfully logged in when valid registered email and correct password are entered
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Login screen
A registered account exists with known credentials
Device has an active internet connection

steps.content: Navigate to the Login screen
steps.expected: Login screen is displayed with email and password fields

steps.content: Enter the registered email address in the email field
steps.expected: Email is entered without inline errors

steps.content: Enter the correct password in the password field
steps.expected: Password is entered and masked

steps.content: Tap the Login / Submit button
steps.expected: Form is submitted; a loading indicator is shown

steps.content: Observe the post-login state and measure the time taken
steps.expected: The user is navigated to the main dashboard or home screen; login completes within 3 seconds
